column1: # M7 slice 3a — /Graph X and /Graph A..F use POINT to pick ranges.
# Each successful POINT stores the range in the current graph's
# corresponding slot and returns to READY. /GQ also returns to READY.
# Seed A1..A5 and B1..B5 so the viewport shows them (though the
# actual values don't matter — only the range does).
# Before any Graph command, all slots are empty.
# /GX enters POINT at A1; DOWN x4 extends to A5; Enter commits.
# Move pointer to B1 then /GA selects B1..B5 for series A.
# X is still set from before.
# /GQ exits the /Graph menu to READY without changing state.
